Vermillion is a curvy, six feet four nuka with an angelic figure. She has a round face with luscious, dark lips just waiting for you to be kissed. She’s got white eyelids slightly gleaming with blueish-purple energy, thin veils hiding almond eyes that shine with a serene but somewhat domineering intensity. A fancy pair of glasses frames her angular face and lends her features a protective, almost motherly air. Most of her scalp and head tentacle are covered by a flowing, gilded wimple that reinforces her elegant figure. You also notice the definition of a metallic circlet under her chaste veil, certainly a tikku ornament or psionic amplifier. Overall, the fervent nuka’s face is beautiful to look at despite the malicious air given by her odd skin tone. A polished collar adorned with a stylized cog locks her headgear in place and seals her gothic power armor tight against her curvaceous assets and silky robes. Glossy black lipples emerge from her reinforced plate, tasting the air and blowing you kisses between a few licks. The polished shell protects most of her upper body, legs, and tail in a thic[silly |c |k ] layer of circuitry, force multipliers, and dense metal. Behind her back, you notice a jetpack-like apparatus that generates shimmering, golden hardlight wings that chime melodically whenever the kind nun flaps them. The hardlight projectors are deactivated right now, leaving Vermillion free to bounce around on powerful, hooved legs. Right under her humming backpack, you also spot a pair of gloves-like weapons equipped with coiled cannons — maybe a flamethrower or plasma gun. A belt with pouches and satchels hugs her waist tight. She might not have much skin exposed, but it’s the same dark red as her face — a quite unsettling color that gives her a fiery, impish allure eons away from her naive and kind manners. Bioluminescent marks shine through her habit’s fabric, hinting that her body bears a lot of glow- this planet’s particular brand of taint. Her scarlet G-cups, plush thighs, well-defined derriere, and pudgy belly give her a motherly look her religious garment can’t hope to conceal. You also spot black, glossy, arm-length latex gloves and thigh highs fastened with bronze and gold circlets under her robes. A meaty, tapered tail sprouts from her back and sways merrily behind her. The unmistakable generators and armor covering it leave no doubt about her dangerousness. A large bulge in her dress betrays the presence of a [flags.VERMI_TLK_HERSELF |veiny, twenty inches long horsecock |meaty sausage ] and a pair of plump, grapefruit-sized gonads. She’s got a welcoming, rubbery womanhood glistening with nasty juices well-hidden behind her hefty balls just waiting to be loved. Last but not least, she’s got a puckered asshole waiting for you between her beautiful buns. “I don’t need psionic powers to know what’s on your mind. My blessed body is at your service, my child,” the crimson angel teases, blowing you kisses with her chest-mounted lips.

“Can you tell me more about yourself? [flags.VERMI_TLK_HERSELF |It’s been a while since our last meeting |I don’t think I’ve seen anyone like you before ].” The nuka nun nods. “Of course my child. What do you want to know about myself?” “Your origins to start? How were you raised and who’s the woman behind the veil?” “There’s not a lot to tell, to be honest. I was raised in the Radwood, with the Evermind’s flock. I don’t know who my parents were. My adoptive father found my egg in the deepest, darkest parts of our Goddess’s bountiful garden — I’ll let you imagine the surprise on his face when I hatched in his hands.” An adopted nuka? That’s a surprising background. Are her close ones also involved in the tech cult operating in the area? “My family serves the Order of Our Martyred Wires, the Evermind’s scavengers and food providers. I grew up surrounded with divrani brothers and sisters that never remarked on my peculiar skin tone. One of the higher clerics suspects it might be a very rare case of nuka albinism but our scholars didn’t find anything akin to it in my blood, so my predicament is still a mystery to me — even the many nuka I met through my travels were none the wiser.” You slowly tilt your head. “Your travels? Are you a missionary?” The shapely paladin giggles. “Excuse me my child, a glowing thought crossed my mind. To answer your question, I’m more like an adventurer or ruins raider. Don’t get me wrong, I’ll gladly bring the Evermind’s brilliant light to whoever wants to find shelter under her bountiful aura, but my duties are entirely different,” she says before showing you a small map of your surroundings on her ancient datapad. “The Order of Our Martyred Wires — the division I belong to — is tasked with investigating each of the [var0 |mutated glades |tainted derelicts ] and identifying what scourged it. Depending on how dire the corruption is, it’s up to us to decide how the place must be secured, contained, or protected before someone weaker to the glow than I can be injured — or worse — by the dangerous spot. It’s not an easy or safe job but I wouldn’t trade places for all of the sweet lollipops in the world,” she says, tapping a bag full of treats attached to her belt. “It sounds like a very dangerous job. Were you trained specifically for it?” “No, I was supposed to become a field plow- I mean worker — with my other siblings, but one of the dragon warriors picked a few of us to pass tests. To my surprise, I was deemed worthy of serving in the Evermind’s holy guard and had to go through a rough training routine to prepare my body and soul to the dangers of the Glass. My family is always worried for my sake when I leave our Goddess’s verdant shelter, but this is a sacrifice I’m more than willing to make, if it means my siblings and their offspring won’t see the horrors dwelling under the arid rocks and stained sands.” Weird that they’d recruit the only atypical nuka they had into their militia, especially since she wasn’t meant to be a fighter to begin with. Does she know why she was taken and given these tasks? Vermillion nods. “I was integrated in the Order of Our Martyred Wires because of my resistance to the glow and psionic abilities. Not only can I withstand the highest amounts of corrupting waves, but I can also use my powers to channel the Evermind’s might and defend myself against the hyraxi’s witchboys, the shalinae’s shamans, and my own race’s psykers. I’m also very good at tail whips, but that’s a story for another time.” “Thanks to my gift, I can go to the deepest, darkest, most glow-touched places to recover pre-Glass weapons and relics before people with ill intents do and use them against us. I’m a knight-errant, amateur engineer, and self-taught archeologist condensed into a merciful nuka. Today, for example, I recovered an ancient targeting system that was salvaged by a hyraxi warband. It was quite easy to knock them out in the dark, but I still can’t understand why they used satellite-grade electronics to customize their rig’s hooter,” the puzzled priestess mumbles. “Do you miss your family?” Vermillion reaches for her twinkling datapad and swipes a couple of times up before raising her eyes on you. “I miss them a lot. Sometimes I’m away from them for months, so I keep some trinkets and souvenirs of them to stay strong. The Evermind’s love can’t fill all the holes their absence leaves in my heart,” the armored cleric admits before showing you a picture of her surrounded by about a dozen adult divrani, ranging from light yellow to dark green. “My parents are here,” she says, pointing at a couple of more wrinkled reptiles on the left, “their families are fruit gatherers and farmers in the Radwood since before the Glass. All my other siblings are too, except Saku,” she adds, pointing at the only yellow divrani girl clad in robes. “She joined the temple a year before me and quickly became a very skilled techie. I think she claimed a rusty shipwreck for herself somewhere in the wastes. I hope she’s alright, since it’s on hyraxi lands.” Looking at the picture closely, you notice something in Vermillion’s hands, something fuzzy and with big, brown eyes. “It’s Firmin, my sweetheart, the light of my life, my baby boy. I hope he’s eating well and making lots of friends.” Wait, that’s her child? You didn’t expect her to be a mother — and of a hyraxi boy, no less! “I found him in a cave north of Nuka Town. I was about to leave the glow-infested tunnels when I heard something coming from the other side. Listening to my instincts, I followed the cries, expecting a trap from cruel bandits or from a heartless AI ready to do anything to satiate their demonic thirst for blood. Can you imagine my surprise when I found a tiny, famished hyraxi boy wrapped in rags? I did my best to feed him the rations I had and make him drink some water before looking for his parents, but there were no traces of blood, footsteps, or wheels left in the dusty ground. I flew over the canyons for hours with him nested against my belly in search for a [silly |yeen |hyraxi ] settlement in vain. Since I couldn’t find his genitors, I brought him to my mother and spent a couple of months pampering him. He’s been growing a lot lately,” she adds, showing you a couple more pics of her adopted son and her together. He must be about seven years old now, going by his looks. It’s a strange coincidence that the adopted girl ended up adopting a little boy too. “He’s looking good,” you agree. “An adorable ray of sunlight. I wonder what led his parents or tribe to abandon him.” Vermillion shrugs. “Life on this planet is rough and unforgiving. Few can withstand its cruelty and fewer can live comfortably here. According to father, his ancestor was the concubine of a hyraxi warmonger tasked to organize her warband while she and her sisters pillaged the settlements near the mountains. Father thinks that’s why his bloodline is less individualistic and more organized than the other divrani. His species isn’t one to build fortresses and raise civilizations. Divrani villages are extremely rare and familial structures even rarer, but I’m very glad to be his adopted daughter. I’ll see them again when I make my report to the temple. I hope Firmin will love the roasted shalin seeds I got for cleansing a robot-infested shrine,” she says. Before she can elaborate further, a weird glow under her clothes earns your attention. Is her body riddled with taint? “We nuka can regulate and store the insidious corruption better than others, but even I can’t leave unscathed,” she admits. “The more glow I store, the stronger my powers become. But it makes me have very impure thoughts — especially since I recovered this Emberian upgrade from one of the ships the Evermind’s dragon destroyed,” she adds, lifting her robes to reveal a large, animalistic sheathe and a pair of musky, scarlet orbs dangling in front of an extra wet pussy. “As soon as I got it, I asked to see the Evermind for an exorcism. The Goddess told me she loved my new adornment and that it suited her righteous daughter well, so I kept it and used it since then to relieve pent-up suitors and spread our Mistress’s love,” she says, a very deep blush darkening her already crimson cheeks. “Good to know. Maybe I’ll need your services sooner or later,” you tease. The serene nun smiles. “And I’ll happily deliver. Is there anything else this faithful paladin can do for you?”

“Your outfit is really fancy, where did you get it?” The red nuka’s cheeks take a dark, garnet blush. “Thank you, my child. I put a lot of effort into maintaining my vestment and keeping my protections in stellar shape. I won’t lie to you, it’s a bit ramshackled and tweaked, but it’s really coming together. The priests at the temple and the Evermind were very generous with my gear.” Wait, that’s standard issued gear for her people? “That’d be wonderful, but no. I was referring to the opulent amount of fabric covering my body. My oathsisters and oathbrothers’ robes are usually far more revealing, but I asked if I could get a more chaste one. They also provided me with a few capacitors and batteries to fuel my first shocking gloves and crackling tail whip. I designed a small, primitive exoskeleton with Saku to help me carry my wargear around more easily — I looked a lot more like a hyraxi warmonger’s lieutenant rather than one of the Evermind’s faithfuls,” she giggles. “Wait, I might still have an old pic of it,” she adds before reaching for her ancient datapad. She swipes a dozen times then shows you a slightly pixelated picture of her sister and her standing in their respective gears. Her apparatus of the time looks like a rough conglomerate of thin, bronze plates and archaic cylinders; it’s a miracle she could walk with that many capacitors strapped to her back. “I really rocked the tin can look, didn’t I?” “[pc.kmhbb |Indeed |A really meaty tin can |In more than one way |Yeah! A sexy, shiny tin can |Yeah! A sexy, sturdy tin can ]. I see your sister was a scythe user,” you remark. Vermillion nods. “I used to, as well. We were field plowe-workers before, so we took weapons that we knew we could use proficiently. My trainers and I discovered that I was a mean boxer so I changed career,” she says, punching an invisible opponent. “I still have a radium scythe in my package, in case I really need to finish a downed machine. Saku’s something of an exhibitionist, so she’s always scantily-clad, especially with her wastes-striding gear. It’s a miracle we were allowed to keep and use our findings.” So these elaborate armor and weapons aren’t common? How did she find them? “Correct. I don’t know what my plasma caster used to be, but I know for sure that a hyraxi warchief used similar plasma belchers and cannons to wreak havoc through the shimmering canyons. I remember how big and brutal her ATVs were, spewing disgusting, dark fumes all around the glassed passes. Luckily, the Evermind’s benevolent eyes were on me when I ambushed them. The skies wept all over the narrow rift, bogging her heavy vehicles down long enough to sneak around and pacify her warboys, one after the other. She unleashed a proverbial inferno of electromagnetic fire on me, but I managed to swipe her off her feet with my tail and knock her out using the power our Goddess lent to me. I recovered her dangerous relic and retrofitted it to the bracers on my fulgur fists,” she says, showing you a couple of reinforced metal gloves lined with coils and branded with a cog symbol on top of it. “Fighting all those hyraxi raiders was surely scary,” you remark. “It’s a miracle you came away unscathed.” Vermillion nods before making the cog symbol with her hands. “I was frightened. Fortunately, the Evermind’s might flowed through me and carved a safe road for me. This mission would have been far easier with my power armor but alas, it was one of my first trips and I only discovered it years later.” Yeah, that could have come up handy. Can she tell you how she got it? “Certainly. About two years ago, a bright, green light emerged from the desert in the middle of the night. It’s not unusual, considering how many cities were swallowed underground by the fallout, electromagnetic storms, and tectonic collapses — sometimes their electric grid comes back up and illuminates the Glass with hypnotic colors. This time, it was different. Ancient, rusted machines crawled out of the cursed depths and started to annihilate any form of life they came across. Feeling the danger rise, the temple made a temporary alliance with the local tribes and nomads to turn their assets to shreds. Long story short, we lost a lot of oathsisters and oathbrothers down there, either to the glow or to the merciless, tireless, relentless androids. A couple of shalinae hunters and I found a hidden weapon cache near their main monolithic factory. They took arms that looked like their ancestors’ holy wargear while I recovered a dusty piece of armor.” “Did any of them survive too?” The nun smiles, a sad, forced smile. “Yes, but one of them was greatly-injured and lost his ability to fly. I was almost vaporized by a ray of sick, demonic light, but my newly-acquired defenses tanked almost everything thanks to the unstable shield overcharge I accidentally caused,” she says. “I was never good with polarities. In the end, we reached the inside of the factory and the giant, liquid metal monstrosity that controlled it. This malleable abomination took the shape of a pre-Glass president, albeit a very muscular one. ‘The Insanator’ it called itself,” she whispers, shaking with fear and making intricate moves you suspect are a silent mantra. “To this day, I don’t know how we beat it, but I remember the gold-feathered huntress driving her singing sword through its core and ripping its source out with crackling talons. We cleansed this place with shalinae, nuka, divrani, and hyraxi rituals, then buried our fallen friends together before the truce’s end.” “Do you know if they are still alive? It sounds like you will never see each other again.” “The gold-feathered huntress pronounced vows with a shaman and settled inside a hallowed hollow somewhere south from here. I meet her from time to time. She makes the meanest mixes of roasted seeds and dried fruits around. As for the injured warrior, he returned to the prismatic mountains with his tribe. He came to the temple to ask for our help for an exorcism a year ago. Saku and I followed him through the sharp peaks in the hope to take down a haywire drone,” she replies, showing you a blurry picture of her sister, a burned shalinae, and her posing in front of a downed, manta-like machine. “Couldn’t the Evermind take it down with her anti-aircraft cannons? She could have easily neutralized it,” you remark. The crimson paladin shakes her head. “It swooped too low and had countermeasures ensuring that none of her mighty skylances could hit it. Anything that flew too high immediately became one of its targets. Saku discovered a warehouse full of wing packs, jetpacks, and other high mobility wargear near its lair. Surprisingly, all of them were compatible with my power armor, as if they were designed to be used together. With it, I flew through the ragged mountain range on magnificent wings high enough to lure the corrupted demon into my sister’s electromagnetic trap. As soon as its engines were neutralized, she removed its control AI and disconnected the corrupted avionics. Flying for the first time felt so good, even with a rapacious creature on my trail.”

. How did the story end by the way? “With the mad machine down, the locals organized a big feast to thank us for our help. Saku was allowed to keep some of the fancy, revealing armors and I was allowed to keep my glowing wings by the avian tribe. We brought them back to the temple and had them reviewed by the Evermind. We couldn’t be happier when the Goddess deemed us worthy of keeping them,” the armored nuka squeals, flapping her heavy backpack’s beautiful, projected feathers. She really loves her equipment as much as her family and adopted kid it seems! “One last question. Can you keep it or do you need to return it to the temple once your duties are over?” “Our equipment belongs to the Evermind, so it’s only fair that we maintain it and bring it back in pristine shape. She keeps a very strictly-ordered inventory of her hoarded goods and only gives her servants the relics and technology they’ll need to complete their missions. Since my tasks are very dangerous and risky, I’m only allowed to bring and carry the stuff I made or found. The day I’ll retire, I’ll offer my armor, wings, and gauntlets to the Goddess’s chromed pile and leave it here until somebody else needs it to protect us from the horrors lying under the glassed sands. There’s so many wondrous devices and relics in the temple, I hope you’ll have the chance to gaze at our Lady’s greatness and treasures,” she adds, a serene smile on her face’s impish canvas.

“Can you tell me more about your Goddess? I’d like to know as much as possible before meeting her.” The red nuka makes the cog symbol with her hands. “The Evermind protects. She’s a bright beacon of light and hope shining over these [var0 |hallowed grounds |glow-touched sands ]. I don’t even have words to describe her magnificence, her resplendence, her sublime allure and benevolent gaze — just thinking about her makes me shiver,” Vermillion admits, a blissful smile on her face. A luminous halo forms around her, a golden crown of sunlight[pc.cl kineticist | induced by her eldritch powers that ] hugs her exposed face in a shimmering mask of holiness. It’s almost terrifying to see how her faith manifests and fuels her abilities. “So... what is she like? Is she a divrani? A nuka? Something else altogether?” “I can’t describe her without insulting or diminishing her beauty. All I can say is that she’s a magnanimous entity. She always gives people a chance to prove their worth in a divine ordeal of her choice, while all the other leaders have pretty brutal societal systems in comparison. Miscreants in nuka territory must either serve a sentence in jail or walk the demon-riddled wastes for a year to be forgiven. Those convicted in shalinae grounds must be forgiven by their ancestors and tribal gods through ritual combat to earn their place back in society — most of them lead to death or permanent injuries for the parties involved. Last but not least, the hyraxi warbands just enslave anyone that approaches their territories unsolicited. There’s supposedly some stand-alone settlements with their own rules and with more racial and cultural diversity, but they struggle day-to-day just to keep their independence and freedom from the bigger, meaner warmongers.” You tilt your head, intrigued by her explanation. “What’s the Evermind’s policy concerning her neighbors?” “It’s complicated. The Goddess doesn’t deal with other settlements except if they try to invade the Radwood or if they find some form of ancient technology. In the latter’s case, she sends a missionary to them to identify the machine in question and decide what to do with it. We either have to make a mundane report back to the temple if it’s a trivial tool, or take direct action if it represents a big enough threat to anyone bathed in the Evermind’s light. In these cases, we either teach them the correct way to use their findings, trade it for an equivalent, safer device, or we confiscate it and seal it in our sanctum’s depths. Some of us also handle tainted objects. We place them in biohazard canisters then store them in bunkers spread across the irradiated sands,” she adds, passing her fingers over one of the glowing marks gleaming from under her robes. So she isn’t to be messed with. How is she with people? Is she kind? Cruel? Impatient? The nuka nun shakes her head. “The Goddess is curious, so she tries to learn a lot from her guests. I’ve seen her intrigued by very common things while intricate designs didn’t make her bat an eye. Most of the time, she’s patient, especially to younger ones. I once brought my little Firmin with me so that he could see his momma’s beautiful boss — the Evermind found him so cute and pure that she allowed him to give her a big kiss, something only her most favored acolytes can usually do without facing consequences. My baby boy was so happy and excited he did an all-nighter thinking about what kind of present he could offer her,” she giggles. “I’m sure she’ll be kind to you as well. I’ve only seen her annihilate someone in holy fire once, and that was because the demonic-possessed creature in question tried to poison the Radwood and its inhabitants with banemoths.[pc.ra mothrine | You should be fine, don’t worry. ] Her Holiness will love to meet a worthy Emberian, I’m sure of it,” she babbles, fully aware that her boss might be dubious about another outsider asking for her favors.

“I’ve got something for you.” The red nuka tilts her head. “An offering? I — and the Evermind — will be very pleased to know that somebody made these [var0 |hallowed grounds |taint-stained sands ] a safer place. The slightest relic — if handled incorrectly — can become an engine of destruction in the wrong hands. I’ll be sure to reward you for your sacrifice.”

“Thank you so much for your gifts, [pc.name]! I’ll be sure to tell everyone how selfless and good you were,” the [silly |scarlet |crimson ] nun giggles before dropping a big smooch on your cheek. “Here, take this. It’s not much but this magic ore will grant you the Evermind’s strength,” she adds, handing you a polished shard of savicite[var0 | and a raw, gleaming piece of adjite ]. A great feeling of warmth[pc.cl kineticist | and power ] takes you over as you put Vermillion’s rewards in your inventory. Last but not least, the ruby servant offers you her serene smile, a sincere, luminous treat that shines bright over these wretched lands. “Is there anything else you need me for, my child?”

“I could use some help to relieve my body and soul from some burning ailment,” you tease. The [silly |scarlet servant |crimson paladin ]’s cheeks take a deep garnet. “O-Of course my child. I see I’m not the only one affected by the glow-tainted thoughts, especially now that I’ve got a virile scepter between my legs. There’s moments the voices are so loud and powerful that I struggle to stay in control — my Goddess would be very disappointed if she knew one of her trusted missionaries behaved like a rabid, debased beast instead of bringing her light to the world.” Before you can do anything, she stops you with a raised hand and fetches a green pill from one of her pouches. “Eat this first. It’s a mix of proteins and refined fruits grounded with selenium[silly | - the psionic rahn raider wasn’t hurt in any way to make this drug ].
